steps.title: Find the probability assigned to group C
steps.content:
Since every participant belongs to exactly one group, the probabilities must sum to 1:
$$P(A) + P(B) + P(C) = 1$$
Substituting the given probabilities:
$$0.48 + 0.24 + P(C) = 1$$
$$P(C) = 1 - 0.48 - 0.24 = 0.28$$

steps.title: Multiply the probability by the total to find the count
steps.content:
The number of participants in group C is the probability times the total number of participants:
$$\text{Number in C} = 0.28 \times 325$$
$$= \frac{28}{100} \times 325 = \frac{9100}{100} = 91$$
That matches the expected answer - 91 participants are assigned to group C.
